task_description: Create a new folder, open a command-line session in it, and initialize it as a version control repository.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a yellow folder desktop icon labeled "file1" with a semitransparent red X centered directly on the folder graphic, indicating that exact icon is the target. In the full-screen desktop view, this same "file1" folder appears by itself near the upper-right area of the Windows 11 desktop, separate from many other icons arranged in columns on the left.
caption.action: Double-click the desktop folder icon labeled "file1".
caption.expectation: The folder should open in a File Explorer window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the File Explorer command bar near the top of the folder window, with a semitransparent red X centered on the button labeled "新建" with a plus sign icon, indicating that exact toolbar control is the target. A tooltip above it says a new item can be created in the current location. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder named "file1", showing several files in the main list and the left navigation pane on the side.
caption.expectation: A new-item menu should open, showing options for creating a folder or file in the current directory.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the new-item dropdown menu from File Explorer, with a semitransparent red X centered on the top menu item labeled "文件夹" with a yellow folder icon, indicating that exact menu entry is the target. In the full-screen view, the File Explorer window for the folder "file1" is open, the new-item menu is expanded beneath the toolbar, and the desktop is visible behind it.
caption.action: Click the "文件夹" (Folder) option in the new-item menu.
caption.expectation: A new folder should be created in the current directory and its name should become editable for immediate renaming.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the file list in File Explorer with a newly created folder name in inline edit mode. The name field is highlighted with a blue selection box and contains the text "tes", indicating the current temporary folder name is being edited and the typed text is replacing the default selected name rather than appending elsewhere. In the full-screen view, the File Explorer window is open to the folder "file1", showing existing items such as ".git", "package.json", and several text files, with the new folder row selected near the top of the list.
caption.action: Type "TEST" into the selected new folder name field.
caption.expectation: The folder's editable name should update to "TEST" in the inline rename box, ready to be confirmed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the top portion of the File Explorer window and surrounding desktop; a semitransparent red X is visible, but the raw action is a keyboard action, so the relevant state is that the folder rename is ready to be confirmed. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder "file1", and a newly created folder is selected in the file list with its name field showing "test" in an inline edit box with a yellow folder icon to its left.
caption.action: Press Enter to confirm the folder name.
caption.expectation: The inline rename box should close, and the new folder should remain in the list with its name saved as "test".

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the file list in File Explorer with a semitransparent red X centered on the selected row labeled "test," which has a yellow folder icon on the left, indicating the target is the folder item itself rather than blank space or text being edited. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder "file1" and the item list includes ".git," "test," "package.json," and several text files; the "test" folder row is highlighted as the current selection.
caption.expectation: The "test" folder should open, and File Explorer should navigate into that folder to show its contents.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered in a plain white empty area, with no file, folder, icon, text label, or menu under it, indicating the target is blank whitespace inside the folder contents pane. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the folder path ending in "test"; the main file list area is empty and displays the message meaning the folder is empty, with column headers above and no context menu currently visible. This means the right-click occurs on empty space in the folder view, where a context menu would typically contain background actions such as view/sort/group, refresh, and paste or new-item options rather than actions for a specific file.
caption.action: Right-click the empty white space inside the folder contents area.
caption.expectation: A background context menu should appear for the folder view, showing general options like view/layout and sorting controls, refresh, paste if available, and new item creation actions.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the bottom entry of a File Explorer background context menu, with the semitransparent red X centered on the menu item labeled "显示更多选项" (Show more options). It is a menu command row with a small icon at the left, not blank space. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open inside the empty folder "test," and a right-click context menu is already open over the blank contents area; the menu includes items such as view, sort, group by, refresh, new, properties, and the bottom "Show more options" entry.
caption.action: Click the "显示更多选项" (Show more options) item at the bottom of the context menu.
caption.expectation: The current compact context menu should be replaced or expanded into the classic full context menu with additional folder background options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a classic File Explorer context menu, with the semitransparent red X centered on the menu item labeled "在线端中打开(T)". This is a menu command row with a small black terminal-style icon on the left, located above "Open with Visual Studio" and below the undo rename entry. In the full-screen view, File Explorer is open to the empty folder "test," and the expanded background context menu is visible over the blank contents area, indicating this is a menu command in the folder background context menu rather than a file item.
caption.action: Click the context menu item labeled "在线端中打开(T)".
caption.expectation: That menu command should be activated, the context menu should close, and a terminal or command-line window associated with this folder should open.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a Windows PowerShell terminal with a black background and white text. At the command prompt, the current line reads "PS C:\Users\13929\Desktop\file1\test> g" with the text cursor immediately after the lowercase letter g, indicating text is being entered at the prompt and will append to that existing g rather than replace it. A Chinese input candidate bar is floating below the prompt line. In the full-screen view, the PowerShell window is in front of an open File Explorer window for the folder named "test" on the Windows desktop.
caption.action: Type "GIT" at the PowerShell command prompt, appending it to the existing text already on the line.
caption.expectation: The prompt line should update to include the newly typed characters, forming a longer command beginning with the existing "g" text.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image is a dark, nearly black area with no distinct control visible under the overlay, which matches a keyboard action rather than a clickable target. In the full-screen view, a Windows PowerShell tab is open in Windows Terminal in front of File Explorer. At the prompt, the current command line reads "PS C:\Users\13929\Desktop\file1\test> g" with the text cursor immediately after the lowercase g, and a Chinese input candidate bar is floating below the prompt with candidates including "git".
caption.expectation: The current input should be submitted at the PowerShell prompt, causing the terminal to execute the typed command and display the next output or an error message.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the Windows PowerShell prompt on a black terminal background. The current command line reads "PS C:\Users\13929\Desktop\file1\test> git ini" with the text cursor immediately after "ini", and a small Chinese input candidate popup is visible below showing "1 ini", indicating text is being entered and will append to the existing command rather than replace it. In the full-screen view, the Windows Terminal window is centered in front of a File Explorer window open to the folder named "test" on the desktop.
caption.action: Type "INIT" at the PowerShell prompt, appending it after the existing "git ini" text.
caption.expectation: The command line should update from "git ini" to "git init", with the cursor moving to the end of the completed command.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image is a dark terminal area with a semitransparent red X, but the raw action indicates a keyboard action. In the full-screen view, a Windows PowerShell tab is open in Windows Terminal on top of File Explorer. At the prompt, the command line reads "PS C:\Users\13929\Desktop\file1\test> git init" with the text cursor at the end of "init". A small Chinese input candidate popup is visible below the command line showing "1 initial".
caption.expectation: PowerShell should execute the "git init" command and display the initialization result, typically creating a new Git repository in the current folder.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image is a dark terminal area with a semitransparent red X, but the raw action indicates a keyboard action rather than a click. In the full-screen view, Windows Terminal is in front of File Explorer, showing a Windows PowerShell tab with the prompt line reading "PS C:\Users\13929\Desktop\file1\test> git init" and the cursor at the end of the command on a black background.
caption.expectation: PowerShell should run the "git init" command and display the repository initialization output, returning to a new prompt afterward.
